$comment: Canonical transition trigger enum + per-activity-type required wiring. Read by the LLM (during workflow generation) and by WorkflowJsonValidator.foreach_wiring_incomplete. The validator does not yet enforce per-activity-type combinations for non-loop nodes — keep that data here so it is ready to enforce in future.

triggers.name: ON_SUCCESS
triggers.emittedWhen: Activity completed without an errorClass. Default forward edge.

triggers.name: ON_NO_MATCH
triggers.emittedWhen: All conditional ON_SUCCESS edges evaluated to false. The default-priority-100 ON_NO_MATCH edge is the fall-through. Used as the loop-exit edge for foreach/while/batch.

triggers.name: ON_ERROR
triggers.emittedWhen: Activity returned a non-VALIDATION / non-TIMEOUT errorClass (TOOL, EXPRESSION, UNCAUGHT, SUBFLOW).

triggers.name: ON_VALIDATION_ERROR
triggers.emittedWhen: Activity failed input/output schema validation, or a SpEL expression failed to evaluate (ErrorClass.VALIDATION).

required.description: From the foreach activity to the first body activity (the iteration step).
From the LAST body activity back to the foreach activity. Without this back-edge the loop runs exactly one iteration.
From the foreach activity to the activity that follows the loop. MUST be isDefault=true and priority>=100 so it fires only after the last iteration sets __loop_continue_<id>=false.

syntheticVariables: __loop_continue_<activityId> (boolean, written by dispatcher, read by RouteResolver to suppress the body-forward edge after exhaustion)

required.description: From while to the first body activity.
From the last body activity back to the while activity.
From while to the activity after the loop. Fires when the condition evaluates false.
From batch to the first body activity.
From the last body activity back to the batch activity.
From batch to the activity after the loop.

constraints: pluginName MUST be null (the activity is not a plugin; the engine routes to its own LLM call).
outputVariables[] must declare at least one variable.

Outgoing transitions either use ON_SUCCESS with a SpEL condition (XOR/exclusive gateway) or fan out to multiple ON_SUCCESS edges (AND-split). Receivers of an AND-split must set andJoin=true on the join node.
